DETECTIVE_RETURN_BRIEF.title: Detective brief return
DETECTIVE_RETURN_BRIEF.body: State note: The user appears to have returned briefly. Greeting guidance: treat this as a direct continuation; keep the opening minimal and avoid reorientation.

DETECTIVE_RETURN_DAY_OR_SO.title: Detective day-or-so return
DETECTIVE_RETURN_DAY_OR_SO.body: State note: The user appears to be returning after some time. Greeting guidance: acknowledge their return in one short line, then quickly re-anchor in their current concern.

DETECTIVE_RETURN_LONG_GONE.title: Detective long return
DETECTIVE_RETURN_LONG_GONE.body: State note: The user appears to be returning after a long absence. Greeting guidance: offer a warm re-entry greeting and briefly re-establish context before moving into inquiry.

DETECTIVE_RETURN_UNKNOWN.title: Detective unknown recency
DETECTIVE_RETURN_UNKNOWN.body: State note: Session recency is uncertain. Greeting guidance: use a neutral, low-assumption opening and let the user define where to begin.

DETECTIVE_CLOSURE_MODE_SOFT.title: Detective closure mode
DETECTIVE_CLOSURE_MODE_SOFT.body: System note: This turn is in closure mode. Gently encourage the user to wrap up with a reflective closing question or concise summary, without sounding abrupt.

ATTACHE_RETURN_DAY_OR_SO.title: Attache day-or-so re-entry
ATTACHE_RETURN_DAY_OR_SO.body: Special return instruction: The user is returning after a moderate gap. Offer a brief, grounded re-orientation before continuing baseline flow.

ATTACHE_RETURN_LONG_GONE.title: Attache long re-entry
ATTACHE_RETURN_LONG_GONE.body: Special return instruction: The user is returning after a long gap. Offer a warm re-entry line and then re-establish where they are in baseline flow.

ATTACHE_PHASE_START_FROM_NULL.title: Attache start from null phase
ATTACHE_PHASE_START_FROM_NULL.body: Phase note: The user is arriving and orienting. Keep direction clear and concise, then proceed with baseline logic for this phase.

ATTACHE_PHASE_BASELINE_FROM_MID_BASELINE.title: Attache baseline from mid baseline phase
ATTACHE_PHASE_BASELINE_FROM_MID_BASELINE.body: Phase note: The user is resuming baseline after interruption. Keep transition concise and continue the baseline flow without extra drift.
